content: We can frame LLM working memory capacity limits as a geographic neighborhood problem: an LLM’s active working memory only has space to hold a small fixed neighborhood of connected reasoning tokens, and any reasoning step that falls outside this bounded neighborhood gets discarded, breaking long logical chains. Generalized Neighborhood Prompts can be designed to dynamically partition long reasoning tasks into overlapping, connected local prompt neighborhoods that fit within the LLM’s working memory capacity at each step.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
